record_kind: HELD NODE — birthed via /intent (WHAT/HOW/trace/shape/falsifier), deliberately NOT cast. Held because the same afternoon produced a lesson about casting rules that are still being re-derived.
what: A nightly process that DRAINS the accumulating stores instead of letting them pile up. Three jobs, one pass: (1) compute YIELD per probe in `questions/` — firings, and of those, how many produced a hypothesis that survived verification — and move tenure (CANDIDATE -> TENURED -> RETIRED) on the measurement, not on age or on who proposed it; (2) evaluate each held node's stated clearing condition and surface the ones that now hold as ripe for /sorted; (3) surface held nodes past their horizon with no movement, because a question blocking work silently is the failure mode the corpus was built to end.
why:
Akien, 2026-07-22: 'if you're going to post feedback to questions (which CAN go in CairnCommons per our conversations), then we will need an overnight process to process them.' Correct, and the sharper form: a store with an intake and no drain is worse than no store, because it produces the APPEARANCE of capture. Filing a red and never working it is indistinguishable from forgetting it, except that it also costs the resolver cycles that produced the red.

And nothing is measuring yield today. Probes have been fired dozens of times from inside skill files and measured zero times, so no probe has ever earned tenure and none has ever been retired for uselessness. T4.1 and T5.1 are not partially done; they are unstarted, and this is what starts them.
how: An OPERATIONAL-DRIVER, not new code: (trigger, method-pointer, why, sources/targets), wiring proven primitives. Trigger = a nightly horizon, which is just a predicate that goes true, evaluated where its data is owned. Targets = `questions/` (tenure writes) and a surfaced digest. Per the class's method-registry gate, every method it points at must already be PROVEN in proven-space — a driver introduces no new code (Law 8). If a needed method is not proven, that is a child, not a reason to write fresh code inside the driver.
trace: T4.1 — 'Question corpus = chartered store with yield tracking.' The store now exists; the tracking does not. This is the tracking.
T5.1 — 'Questions earn tenure by yield; losers retire.' Retirement cannot happen without something that computes the measure and acts on it.
Law 3 — nothing is known until measured. Tenure asserted without a yield computation is a hypothesis wearing a verdict's clothes.
Law 1 — the resolver is spent on the novel. A probe that has stopped yielding is the resolver being spent on the settled, every single firing, forever, until something retires it.
shape: NEW TRACK. Not an aside to the roots/partition work. It is the drain for stores that currently only accumulate, and it is a candidate FIRST MEMBER of `operational-driver` — a class cast 2026-07-17 and defined 2026-07-18 that still has zero members, which by its own invariant (proved + feeds back on failure) means the class itself is unproven. A class with no members has never been tested as a class.
falsifier:
It is WRONG or hollow if, after a month of nights, no probe's tenure has ever changed. Either the yield measure does not discriminate, or the corpus does not vary, or the driver is not actually reading anything — and all three look identical from outside, which is why the check has to be on tenure MOVEMENT rather than on the driver running green.

WRONG-INTENT SIGNAL, the one to watch: if it becomes a nightly digest that Akien has to read, it has MOVED the work rather than done it. The founding intention is to get Akien out of running the system; a process whose output is a human's morning reading list is the ops role reintroduced under a new name. What it surfaces should be ripe FOR /sorted, not ripe for triage.
horizon: First night it runs with a non-empty probe corpus. Measure: did any tenure move, and was any held node correctly surfaced as ripe?
waits_on: The probe corpus being non-empty — its first job is extracting the probes already embedded in /intent and /sorted, which have been asked many times and recorded never. Draining an empty store proves nothing.
not_cast_because: /intent births and traces; it does not cast. Casting binds a gate-set, and the honest sequence is: extract the probes first, so the driver has something real to measure, then cast against a corpus that exists. Casting now would bind gates to a store with zero members — a proof a hollow build could pass (Law 8).
